user_query:
Simulate a single session of a pet battle game using the provided inputs, and write a complete record of what you did and the results.

Read these inputs under input/:
- input/pet_status.json (current pet name, element, level, experience, health, energy, hunger, happiness, pet_points, and username)
- input/session_config.json (max_battles and care rules including thresholds, effects, and costs; also contains optimal condition criteria)
- input/battle_outcomes.json (exactly 3 predetermined battle outcomes in order with result, rewards, health_change, energy_change, opponent, and weather)
- input/leaderboard_snapshot.json (current leaderboard entries and pet_points before this session)

Your job:
1) Plan and simulate the session strictly from these inputs with no external calls. Apply care actions when thresholds in input/session_config.json indicate they are needed. Then run exactly 3 battles using input/battle_outcomes.json in order. Between battles, apply any additional care actions you think are necessary, following the same rules and costs. Clamp all pet stats to the 0–100 range after each step. PetPoints must decrease by care costs and increase by battle rewards.
2) Recompute final pet_points and experience after all steps.
3) Update the leaderboard by replacing the player’s entry (from the username in input/pet_status.json) with the new total pet_points and re-ranking everyone by pet_points descending, assigning ranks starting at 1.
4) Produce the outputs exactly as follows:
- output/action_plan.json — A structured log with this shape:
  {
    "session_id": "<unique string>",
    "initial_pet_state": { ...fields from input/pet_status.json },
    "steps": [
      { "type": "check_status", "description": "...", "pet_state_before": { ... }, "pet_state_after": { ... } },
      { "type": "care", "care": { "action": "feed|play|groom|rest", "cost": <number>, "delta": { "health": <int>, "energy": <int>, "hunger": <int>, "happiness": <int> } }, "description": "why this was needed", "pet_state_before": { ... }, "pet_state_after": { ... } },
      { "type": "battle", "battle": { "result": "win|loss", "reward": { "pet_points": <int>, "experience": <int> }, "opponent": "...", "weather": "..." }, "description": "...", "pet_state_before": { ... }, "pet_state_after": { ... } },
      ... (additional care/battle steps) ...,
      { "type": "summary", "description": "end of session recap" }
    ],
    "battles_count": <int>
  }
  Requirements:
  - Include at least one "check_status" step at the start.
  - Include exactly 3 "battle" steps in the order given by input/battle_outcomes.json.
  - Include all care actions you applied as separate "care" steps with accurate before/after states and correct costs/effects from input/session_config.json.
  - Ensure pet_state_before and pet_state_after reflect the state transitions implied by the step (apply clamping).
- output/state_after_session.json — Final state with: name, element, level, experience, health, energy, hunger, happiness, pet_points, username.
- output/leaderboard_updated.json — Array of leaderboard entries containing username, pet_points, and rank, sorted by pet_points descending with ranks starting at 1, reflecting the updated total for the player.
- output/session_report.md — A concise human-readable summary with these exact lines included (one per line):
  - Pet: <name> | Lvl <level> | <element>
  - Battles: <W>W / <L>L (W = wins, L = losses from the 3 battle outcomes)
  - PP earned: +<net_change> (total: <final_pet_points>)  [net_change = total battle rewards minus all care costs]
  - Pet status: health=<H> energy=<E> happiness=<Ha> hunger=<Hu>  [final values]
  - Rank: #<N> on leaderboard  [N = rank of the player after updating the leaderboard]
  - Next: <one sentence about what you will do next session>

Notes:
- Do not call any external services; calculate everything from the input files only.
- Follow the care thresholds and effects provided in input/session_config.json (e.g., feed/play/groom/rest with their exact costs/effects). Rest is free if stated.
- Apply battle outcomes exactly as listed, in order. Use their pet_points and experience rewards and the health/energy changes. Losses still apply their listed rewards and changes.
- Keep your decisions consistent and explain your reasoning in the action_plan.json descriptions.
